craigslist.title: Gerstle Park - Large One-Bedroom Apartment
craigslist.description: QR Code Link to This Post Apartment for rent on the second floor of a small complex (7 units) in Gerstle Park. The apartment is approximately 750 square feet and the property is well maintained. The living room is large. The bedroom is good sized with a large closet. There are another two storage closets in the hall and off the kitchen. Mostly everything has been replaced - new garbage disposal, fixtures, dishwasher, wall oven, carpet, window coverings and paint. There is a covered parking space under the building and there is an additional storage closet in the parking area. The neighborhood is quiet, is close to transportation, and is within walking distance to 4th street. There is no smoking allowed in the apartment or on the grounds. Everything will be finished between 5/20 and 5/29. Appliances arrive 5/20 and window coverings 5/28. Please call with any questions or to make an appointment to see the apartment.

craigslist.title: Room + private bath in Nob Hill - Furnished /summer / short-term
craigslist.description: QR Code Link to This Post Room + private bath in Nob Hill - furnished / summer / short-term Available now. Furnished bedroom with its own bathroom in a great Nob Hill spot. Around the corner from Trader Joe's and CVS, a few blocks to Polk Street, Huntington Park, and easy transit. The Financial District is about a 10–20 minute walk, so it's a simple commute downtown. Good for a work assignment, internship, relocation, or just a few months in the city. It's a temporary furnished rental, so it fits a few-month stay better than a long lease. Think commuter room, summer stay, or a landing spot while you get settled. What you get: Private bedroom, private bathroom Queen bed All utilities included Fast Wi-Fi TV with Apple TV Washer/dryer in the unit Big backyard with seating Heads up on the kitchen: it's basic. No dishwasher and no full oven, just a toaster oven. Fine if you cook light or lean on the cafes and restaurants nearby, of which there are plenty. You'd share the flat with my wife and me. We're out most of the day, home in the evenings, and mostly keep to our rooms on the far end of the place. We keep the common areas clean and quiet, especially at night, so it suits someone who likes a mellow home base. Two friendly cats live in the common area, so you'll want to be comfortable around cats. No smoking, no pets. Happy to show it in person or over FaceTime. If you reach out, tell me a bit about yourself, what brings you to SF, your ideal move-in date, and how long you're hoping to stay. Thanks! — Tony
